audit_assumptions.audit_type: Full 12-category design, AI-slop, and accessibility audit
audit_assumptions.input: Streamlit source, theme configuration, shared CSS, and all six page structures
audit_assumptions.release_target: Design A, AI-slop A, accessibility A, token compliance >= 90%
audit_assumptions.provenance: Generated by an LLM design-review pass reading the source, not an automated crawler or CI-run tool. Treat these scores as self-assessment, not independent or automated QA sign-off.

findings.description: Page title, decision question, provenance, filter context, and KPI strip use similar visual weight, weakening the first-screen decision path.
findings.fix: Create a compact branded page header and a distinct context strip before the KPI hierarchy.

findings.description: Five or six KPI containers repeat the same native metric treatment without a clear status or benchmark hierarchy.
findings.fix: Replace the KPI strip with a bespoke component that orders label, value, comparison, status, and denominator consistently.

findings.description: The application uses the generic sans-serif theme and relies on Streamlit defaults for most of its type scale.
findings.fix: Apply the specified Source Sans 3 family and explicit heading, body, caption, and numeric-value styles.

findings.description: All tested body, muted, link, button, warning, success, and danger color pairs pass WCAG AA.
findings.fix: Preserve the governed palette and move remaining hardcoded surface colors into shared tokens.

findings.description: The global CSS defines only a handful of refinements, leaving page rhythm, card padding, section separation, and content width mostly implicit.
findings.fix: Implement the documented 4-48px spacing scale and consistent analytical content width across shared surfaces.

findings.description: Nearly every analytical region uses an identical border and shadow, producing box fatigue instead of meaningful grouping.
findings.fix: Use borders for analytical cards, whitespace for section grouping, and elevation only for interactive or selected surfaces.

findings.description: Native widgets provide reliable states, but chart cards and downloadable table regions have little visual response or action grouping.
findings.fix: Add consistent hover, focus-visible, selected, empty, and action-area treatments without changing behavior.

findings.description: The six-page decision flow is clear, but the sidebar lacks a product lockup and stronger separation between navigation, filters, and metadata.
findings.fix: Add a compact brand component and structure filter groups as a SaaS control rail.

findings.description: Only the KPI row has an explicit mobile rule; chart grids, page headers, context rows, and wide tables rely on default reflow.
findings.fix: Add component-level 1280px, 992px, and 768px behavior and visible table overflow affordances.

findings.description: Native controls retain visible labels and all audited semantic color pairs pass AA, but bespoke landmarks and reduced-motion safeguards are absent.
findings.fix: Give custom surfaces semantic headings and roles, retain text status labels, and honor prefers-reduced-motion.

findings.description: The interface avoids decorative motion, but future hover transitions need an explicit reduced-motion fallback.
findings.fix: Keep transitions short and disable them when reduced motion is requested.

findings.description: Decision questions, caveats, units, and empty-state guidance are specific and consistently avoid causal overclaiming.
findings.fix: Preserve the current information density while tightening repeated metadata into shared components.

findings.description: The product avoids generic gradients and marketing copy, but repeated equal cards and untouched framework chrome make the interface feel assembled rather than authored.
findings.fix: Introduce one distinctive, domain-specific visual component system instead of adding decorative gradients or oversized rounded cards.

findings.description: Artifact loading is cached and the dataset is small; visual changes should avoid frontend dependencies and layout-shifting assets.
findings.fix: Use an inline CCv2 component with no build step, remote image, or runtime dependency.

coherence.notes: The analytical language and governed palette are coherent, but hierarchy, spacing, and component identity still depend heavily on Streamlit defaults.
